description: a4/ workspace hooks. Most logic lives in the Python dispatcher `scripts/a4_hook.py` (subcommands: post-edit, stop, user-prompt). Two small bash hooks (`cleanup-edited-a4.sh`, `sweep-old-edited-a4.sh`) handle trivial edited-record tmp-file lifecycle. Session-scoped state under `.claude/tmp/a4-edited/`: `a4-edited-<sid>.txt` (paths edited this session, consumed by Stop) and `a4-resolved-ids-<sid>.txt` (issue ids already announced via UserPromptSubmit, dedupes per-session). PostToolUse records edits and reports per-component status-consistency. UserPromptSubmit resolves `#<id>` references in the prompt to `a4/<type>/<id>-<slug>.md` paths and injects them as `additionalContext` so Claude reads the file directly instead of searching. SessionStart no longer reports workspace-wide status-consistency — run `/a4:validate` (or invoke `validate.py` directly) for a manual sweep. Design principles — language choice, lifecycle symmetry, ordering, non-blocking, output channels — live in `docs/hook-conventions.md`. Status transitions and their supersedes/discarded/revising cascades run synchronously from `scripts/transition_status.py` inside the invoking skill/agent — there are no status-writer hooks.

hooks.description: Record edited a4/*.md and report per-component status-consistency
Validate a4/*.md edited this session; exit 2 on violations to force fix
Delete this session's a4-edited record file
Sweep orphan a4-edited record files older than 1 day
Resolve #<id> references in the prompt to a4/<type>/<id>-<slug>.md paths (per-session dedupe)
